title: Full eval: cited+jurisdiction-scoped+reviewed vs. blank-slate (accuracy/citation/jurisdiction-fit/neutrality)
context: The thesis of Know-Your-Rights is that cited + jurisdiction-scoped + attorney-reviewed content outperforms blank-slate LLM answers on legal accuracy, citation coverage, jurisdiction-fitness, and neutrality. The full M4 eval proves or disproves this thesis on the complete pilot content set (tasks 009-011). If cited+reviewed does not clearly beat blank-slate, the thesis is flagged as failing and the project pauses for reassessment. Depends on tasks 008 and 009.
objective: Implement and run the full cited-vs-blank-slate eval on the pilot content set: run fixture scenarios through both the cited+jurisdiction-scoped+attorney-reviewed content path and a blank-slate LLM path; score each on accuracy, citation coverage, jurisdiction-fitness, neutrality, and non-advice compliance; report the delta. Cited+reviewed must clearly beat blank-slate or the thesis is flagged failing.
acceptanceCriteria: Full eval harness implemented (scripts/eval-grounded.ts extended from task 008) covering the complete pilot content set: at least 20 fixture scenarios across the six tenant-rights topics

Scores each response on five dimensions: accuracy (claim matches attorney-reviewed source), citation coverage (cited vs uncited claim ratio), jurisdiction-fitness (jurisdiction-scoped vs unscoped claim), neutrality (partisan/advocacy framing detected), and non-advice compliance (individualized-advice phrasings detected)
LLM judge scoring implemented with at least one attorney spot-check on a sample of fixtures; scoring methodology documented

Structured eval report produced: per-fixture scores, aggregate delta per dimension, and an overall verdict (PASS: cited+reviewed clearly ahead; FAIL: thesis not confirmed with required remediation steps)
If cited+reviewed does not clearly beat blank-slate on the majority of dimensions, the eval exits non-zero and CI reports THESIS FAIL, blocking M5 work
Eval harness is provider-neutral: LLM client behind a thin adapter; no vendor lock-in
Reviewed by maintainer and a licensed attorney for the spot-check; eval result recorded
output: A merged PR containing the full eval harness and a recorded eval report proving that cited+jurisdiction-scoped+attorney-reviewed content clearly beats blank-slate LLM answers on accuracy, citation, jurisdiction-fitness, neutrality, and non-advice compliance — or a THESIS FAIL verdict blocking further investment.
